story_bible.title: The Forged Map
story_bible.setting: Oslo harbor district, 1923. Fog, gas lamps, dockside warehouses.
world_rules: No omniscient narration — close third-person only.
Each chapter must advance at least one plot thread to a new state.
story_bible.timeline_anchor: Chapter 1 begins on 3 November 1923, a Tuesday.

characters.name: Elin Voss

facts.appearance: Tall, auburn hair worn pinned up. A scar runs along her left jaw from a childhood accident.
facts.role: protagonist — harbor inspector with a past in naval intelligence
facts.motivation: Find out who killed her former partner Knut before the investigation is buried.
secrets: She forged the inspection report that cleared the Halversen cargo six months ago.

relationships.Jonas Lund: uneasy alliance — she trusts him less than she lets on
relationships.Director Marte Brekke: nominal superior; Elin suspects she is complicit

voice_samples: She didn't answer right away. Let the silence do the work.
Elin had learned long ago that the truth doesn't arrive — you have to go in after it.

characters.name: Jonas Lund

facts.appearance: Heavy-set, sandy hair going grey at the temples. Always wears the same brown overcoat.
facts.motivation: Stay alive and keep his daughter out of the mess he's made.
secrets: He knows the map is forged. He commissioned it.

relationships.Elin Voss: nervous respect — she is the only person who might actually stop Brekke

facts.physical_state: exhausted, bruised left hand from a recent altercation

voice_samples: Jonas spread his hands on the table as if surrendering to gravity.
'There are things you don't come back from,' he said. 'This is one of them.'

plot_threads.title: The Forged Harbor Map
plot_threads.summary: A navigational chart used to smuggle contraband past customs has been forged. Elin has the original; someone knows she has it.

plot_threads.title: Knut's Death
plot_threads.summary: Elin's former partner Knut was found in the harbor two weeks ago. Official ruling: accident. Elin doesn't believe it.

plot_threads.title: Director Brekke's Role
plot_threads.summary: Elin suspects her superior is involved but has no evidence yet.

style_guide.content: Spare, precise prose. Short sentences in tense moments. Longer, observational sentences during quieter scenes. No adverb clusters.
Show interiority through action and observation, not internal monologue dumps.
Water and fog are recurring. The harbor is never just backdrop — it mirrors the characters' states.
The warehouse smelled of salt and old rope. Elin kept her hand near her coat pocket and didn't touch anything.

arc_goals.goal: Establish Elin's world and the inciting problem: the forged map surfaces, and she realizes Knut knew about it before he died.
Elin finds Jonas, who is her only lead. Their first meeting establishes mutual distrust but shared necessity.
